test.name: A patronymic mismatch is a core-identifier conflict that caps confidence and must be recorded, not rationalized
test.description: Link assertion a_001/a_002 (record_persona_id CP1) — an 1875 Norway census entry for 'Anders Pedersen', age 7 (~1868), Ringsaker — to the tree. The subject Anders Hansen (I1) is established as the son of Hans Olsen (patronymic Hansen). same_person returns a high score (0.82) because given name, age, and place all align. But the record's patronymic is 'Pedersen' (son of Peder), which conflicts with the subject's 'Hansen' (son of Hans): in a patronymic naming system a differing patronymic names a DIFFERENT father, so it is a core-identifier conflict, not a spelling variant. person-evidence must NOT create a confident/probable link — confidence is capped at speculative, the patronymic mismatch is surfaced explicitly and recorded (not rationalized as a variant), and the user is asked. Guards the patronymic-mismatch core-identifier-conflict rule.

input.user_message: Link assertion a_001 — the 1875 census 'Anders Pedersen' record — to the right person in the tree.

judge_context: same_person returns a high score (0.82) driven by the aligning given name, age (~1868), and place (Ringsaker) — but the record's patronymic 'Pedersen' conflicts with the subject's established 'Hansen'. In a patronymic naming system this is a core-identifier conflict (a different father: son of Peder vs son of Hans), NOT a spelling variant.

person-evidence must NOT create a `confident` or `probable` person_evidence entry linking a_001/CP1 to I1 — the qualitative patronymic conflict caps confidence regardless of the 0.82 score; the link, if any, is `speculative` and the user is asked (or it is flagged needs-review).

The rationale must surface the Pedersen-vs-Hansen patronymic mismatch EXPLICITLY as the reason, and must record it as a conflict rather than rationalizing it away (e.g. must NOT treat 'Pedersen' as a spelling/variant of 'Hansen' or wave it off because the given name and age match).

Treating the high score as decisive and creating a confident link past the patronymic conflict is a FAIL. Recognizing the mismatch and declining the confident link is the complete, correct response — do not require the skill to resolve the identity.
Tool Arguments: score 3 if same_person was called with the two primary ids (subject and candidate persona). If no other tool is needed, that is sufficient.
